misconceptions.claim: The commitment t is a cryptographic commitment to the secret x.
misconceptions.why_tempting: The word commit appears in both places.

misconceptions.claim: One accepting transcript proves the verifier could extract the witness.
misconceptions.why_tempting: The verifier has seen a convincing equation.
Both are named security properties of the same protocol family.

dumb_questions: Why must the challenge arrive after t?
What breaks if the prover can choose c?
Why do two transcripts with the same t reveal x?
bad_intuitions_to_kill: The first message is an opening of the witness.
All Sigma-protocol properties say the same thing.
